resposta:
solution:
```
class Solution:
    def maxTaskAssign(self, tasks: List[int], workers: List[int], pills: int, strength: int) -> int:
        tasks.sort()
        workers.sort()
        i, j, res = 0, 0, 0
        while i < len(tasks) and j < len(workers):
            if workers[j] >= tasks[i]:
                res += 1
                i += 1
                j += 1
            elif pills > 0 and workers[j] + strength >= tasks[i]:
                res += 1
                i += 1
                j += 1
                pills -= 1
            else:
                j += 1
        return res
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n log n + m log m)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Sort tasks and workers in ascending order. Iterate through both arrays, assigning tasks to workers who can complete them. If a worker can't complete a task, try giving them a magical pill if available.
